proof_boundary: This audit verifies local UI/UX rendering, navigation, copy, form-label wiring, route accessibility, and responsive behavior. It does not prove legal, customs, tariff, CFIA, supplier, buyer, payment, hosting, privacy, security, or commercial launch readiness.

findings: Dense packet evidence and policy pages could force page-level horizontal overflow.
Generated operator dashboard tables overflowed mobile viewports.
A long system-health status badge overflowed on mobile.
Several forms needed stable label-control association after render.
Agent API copy still said Dry-Run Tools even though local scoped execution is implemented.

implemented_fixes.change: Added shrink-safe page/grid children, table-contained horizontal scrolling, mobile table cell sizing, and wrapping status badges.
Added a progressive enhancement that associates adjacent labels with visible inputs/selects/textareas and supplies fallback textarea labels.
Renamed Dry-Run Tools to Local Agent Tools to match the no-external-effects local executor contract.
Updated the dashboard renderer so regenerated operator dashboards are mobile-safe and dense tables scroll inside their own containers.

review_notes: Dense evidence, blocker, and operator ledgers remain wide by nature, but now scroll inside their own table containers instead of expanding the whole page.
The UI is review-ready as a local private-beta product surface; external claims and real-world operations remain explicitly gated in the product reports.
